preamble: Monophthongization of the diphthong [ui] to the close back rounded vowel [u] is a common vowel reduction process where the glide element is absorbed into the vocalic part. This process is well-documented in Romance and Germanic languages and represents one of the most frequent diphthong reductions.

examples.note: Monophthongization in word-medial and word-final position, particularly in casual speech and certain dialects.
Reduction of diphthong to single vowel in southwestern Spanish varieties and rapid speech.
Monophthongization in words like 'pursue' where [ui] merges with [u].
Historical monophthongization in Old French → Middle French transition.
